## Current State

The app is minimal — a basic Express server with a `/health` and `/users` endpoint. Here are the improvements I'd recommend, grouped by category:

## 1. Input Validation & Request Handling

**Missing body parsing middleware** — if you ever accept POST/PUT requests, raw request bodies won't be parsed without this, which can cause silent failures or unexpected behavior:

## 2. Security Headers

Add [`helmet`](https://helmetjs.github.io/) to set secure HTTP headers automatically:
```js
const helmet = require('helmet');
app.use(helmet()); // sets X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, CSP, etc.
```

This protects against clickjacking, MIME sniffing, and other common web vulnerabilities with a single line.

---

## 3. Error Handling

There is **no global error handler**. Unhandled errors will expose stack traces or crash the server. Add:
```js
// 404 handler — must come after all routes

});

  console.error(err); // log server-side, never leak stack traces to clients
  res.status(err.status || 500).json({ error: 'Internal server error' });
});
```

---

## 4. Rate Limiting

No rate limiting means the server is vulnerable to brute-force and denial-of-service attacks:
```js
const rateLimit = require('express-rate-limit');

app.use(limiter);

No CORS policy is set. If this API is browser-facing, you should explicitly control which origins are allowed:
